headline: Aetna’s US$117.7m Settlement Puts CVS Health Compliance In Focus
summary: Aetna, a CVS Health subsidiary, has agreed to pay a $117.7 million settlement to the Department of Justice. The settlement addresses allegations that Aetna submitted inaccurate diagnosis codes for Medicare Advantage members to increase reimbursements. The agreement resolves longstanding False Claims Act allegations related to payments from the Medicare Advantage program. For you as an investor looking at CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), this is a sizable legal development tied directly to its...

headline: How The CVS Health (CVS) Story Is Shifting With Medicare Advantage And Valuation Debates
summary: CVS Health’s fair value estimate has been updated from about US$94.84 to roughly US$96.38 per share, a small adjustment that still matters if you are tracking the name closely. This change sits against a backdrop of mixed analyst commentary, with optimism around Medicare Advantage and sector recovery balanced by caution on managed care earnings and capital allocation. Read on to see how these views are shaping the evolving CVS Health story and what to watch next in the analyst...

headline: 3 Value Stocks We’re Skeptical Of
summary: Value stocks typically trade at discounts to the broader market, offering patient investors the opportunity to buy businesses when they’re out of favor. The key risk, however, is that these stocks are usually cheap for a reason – five cents for a piece of fruit may seem like a great deal until you find out it’s rotten.

headline: Occidental Petroleum, CVS, Petco: Top Analyst Calls
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma outlines some of Wall Street's top analyst calls today: Wells Fargo and Piper Sandler upgrading Occidental Petroleum (OXY), Bernstein upgrading CVS (CVS), and Jefferies raising its rating on Petco (WOOF). To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Bernstein upgrades CVS and Cigna, citing PBM reform as key clearing event
summary: Investing.com -- Bernstein upgraded both Cigna Group and CVS Health Corp to Outperform in separate notes on Thursday, arguing that sweeping pharmacy benefit manager (PBM) reforms have removed a major overhang for the sector.

headline: Bernstein Upgrades CVS Health to Outperform, Raises Price Target to $94
summary: Bernstein  analyst Lance Wilkes   upgrades CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) from Market Perform to Outperform and raises the price target from $91 to $94.

headline: Starbucks, Amgen, and CVS Help This Income Fund Shine
summary: Hilda Applbaum, co-manager of the American Funds Income Fund of America, likes to buy inexpensive, high-yielding stocks poised for an upturn. Her strategy has trounced 98% of peers.
